calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Modifying Trust
Off Calendar May-16-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: William Fuller appearing for petitioner. Court proposed to strike out mutual funds and similar forms of funds. Counsel agreed that by striking the two causes that will accomplish what they want to do. Petition is granted in part and denied in part. Counsel will prepare a new order.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Gena Eales CSR# 9176. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Thompson.

Petition For Order Modifying Trust
Off Calendar Apr-02-2007 Continued To May-14-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Modifying Trust
Off Calendar Mar-19-2007 Continued To Apr-02-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Fuller (Counsel for Wells Fargo Bank, Trustee).

Counsel and the Court engaged in colloquy as to the intentions of the Trustor with respect to the nature of the investments in which she intended the trustees to invest and as to whether trust administration was "substantially impaired" (as the term is used in the Probate Code) by the ostensible constraints placed on the trustees by the terms of the Trust. Counsel contended that the increased risk from the want of diversification in the allowed investments constituted substantial impairment. With that in mind, the Court continued the matter to April 2, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in ept 204 to allow counsel to consider whether the investments allowed by Probate Code Section 2574 and California Rules of Court 7.903 (investments allowed to conservators in conservatorship matters) would suffice for the Trustee's purposes.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

Petition For Order Modifying Trust
Off Calendar Mar-05-2007 Continued To Mar-19-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
